steps: Advance Preparation: Aliquot 180 µl seawater medium (e.g. ASW) into each well of a 96-well plate. Keep a log of your plate layout (which samples in which wells). Plate-Setup: a This will result in a 10-fold sample dilution (20µl into 180µl) and is useful for dense cultures that would otherwise lead to "OVER" readings in the fluorometer.
Add 20 µl of sample into the ASW for a 10X dilution. Mix well with a pipette.

Read fluorescence using the Tecan Infinite Pro 200M plate reader. Set up a program using the following wavelengths: excitation 440 nm, emission 680 nm for chlorophyll a; excitation 544 nm, emission 577 nm for phycoerythrin.

Plot fluorescence vs. time to track the real-time progression of cell growth and/or phage infection. Keep in mind that fluorescence is not always a good proxy for cell numbers. Fluorescence can change for a variety of reasons -- physical (e.g. lysis/dissociation), physiological (e.g. nutrient limitation), etc.
creator: Maureen Coleman
